channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Photo-shoot fail: Biden, Trudeau miss traditional photo with world leaders at G20
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — President Joe Biden inadvertently was a missing man in the traditional photo of world leaders at the final Group of 20 summit of his presidency Monday, a photo-shoot fail that U.S. officials attributed to timing.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transgender women to be banned from Capitol Hill female bathrooms under new House GOP proposal
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace is introducing a resolution to ban transgender women from female bathrooms at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon and Elon Musk's SpaceX challenge labor agency's constitutionality in federal court
item.description: Attorneys for Amazon and Elon Musk’s SpaceX argued in a federal appeals court Monday that the National Labor Relations Board’s structure is unconstitutional, advancing a legal fight that may last into the Trump administration where Musk is expected to oversee bureaucratic cost-cutting.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems' Gaetz outrage follows long history of questionable DOJ acts under Biden
item.description: Criticisms have mounted that former Rep. Matt Gaetz as attorney general and would break norms, despite a list of questionable actions taken by DOJ under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania Supreme Court upholds exclusion of certain mail-in ballots, in victory for GOP
item.description: The Keystone State's top court reaffirmed that certain mail-in ballots cannot be counted, in a blow to certain Democrat-led counties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau says Canada 'could have acted quicker' on reining in immigration
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said the federal government in Canada "could have acted quicker" to reign in the massive influx of migrants entering the country following the coronavirus pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Environmental groups present differing opinions of Trump's energy and environment appointees
item.description: Environmental groups are reacting to President-elect Trump's nominees to head energy and environmental issues in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Chris Wright, Trump's choice to run the Energy Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fossil fuel executive Chris Wright, Donald Trump's choice for Energy secretary, is a strong supporter of oil and gas development, including fracking, a key pillar of the president-elect’s quest for U.S. “energy dominance” in the global market.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden asks Congress for about $100 billion in disaster relief funding
item.description: The largest chunk of funding — about $40 billion — would be for FEMA's disaster relief fund, so that it has enough money to last through the coming year. But there are requests for 16 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Republican Eric Hovde concedes defeat to Democrat Tammy Baldwin in US Senate race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin Republican Eric Hovde conceded defeat on Monday to Democratic incumbent Tammy Baldwin in their U.S. Senate race, saying he did not want to "add to political strife through a contentious recount” even though he raised debunked election conspiracies.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Ethics Committee to meet Wednesday amid debate over Gaetz report
item.description: Members of the House Ethics Committee will meet Wednesday, a source confirmed to The Hill, as the panel weighs whether it should release its report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), whom President-elect Trump plans to nominate to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Just before the election, a poll from Selzer sent shockwaves nationwide after it found Vice President Harris leading President-elect Trump in Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied long-range missiles to strike deeper inside Russia. The move eases limitations on the weapons as Russia deploys thousands of North Korean troops to reinforce its war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Big voter turnout this year benefited Republicans, contradicting conventional political wisdom
item.description: The 2024 presidential election featured sky-high turnout, approaching the historic levels of the 2020 contest and contradicting long-held conventional political wisdom that Republicans struggle to win races in which many people vote.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some in the U.S. farm industry are alarmed by Trump's embrace of RFK Jr. and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump won landslide support in much of farm country, but his embrace of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his plan for a tariff fight with China alarms many farmers and agriculture experts.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Trump names attorney Will Scharf to key White House job
item.description: Scharf represented Trump as one of his lawyers during a hearing on presidential immunity before the Supreme Court and was a regular on television defending Trump amid his hush money trial in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Karoline Leavitt to be youngest White House press secretary in history
item.description: Leavitt will be the youngest person to ever hold the job and is expected to deliver press briefings based on Trump’s statement late Friday that he has “utmost confidence she will excel at the podium.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Trump-DeSantis complicated relationship could impact Senate pick: political expert
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis has a big decision to make if Sen. Marco Rubio is confirmed as the next Secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Josh Gottheimer joins NJ governor race
item.description: SOUTH HACKENSACK, N.J. (PIX11) - The race for New Jersey governor thickened Friday as Democratic U.S. Rep. Josh Gottheimer officially threw his hat in the ring.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: President-elect Trump brings South Dakota politicians into national spotlight
item.description: While the next four years of President-elect Donald Trump’s administration are still being constructed, South Dakota politicians are being brought into a larger national conversation.
item.dc:creator: Phil Picardi, Heidi Raschke, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Trump spokesperson Steven Cheung to serve as White House communications director
item.description: Steven Cheung, who has served as a top press aide on the Trump campaign for the past two years, will join the incoming administration as White House communications director, President-elect Trump announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to 'Make America Healthy Again.' He could face a lot of pushback
item.description: RFK Jr. wants to tackle chronic disease. Despite controversial views on vaccines, his focus on healthy food and taking on special interests may find broad support — and face political headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: What Trump's pledge to close Dept. of Education means for students, GOP-led states
item.description: President-elect Trump promised to close the Department of Education. We asked several education policy experts what the impacts of doing so would mean for students and the country.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep and Taylor Haney, MPR News

item.title: What to know North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum, tapped by Trump to run the Interior Department
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum to lead the Interior Department. The two-term Republican governor of the sparsely populated state has taken a business-oriented approach to his tenure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump chooses anti-vaccine activist Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as health secretary
item.description: A longtime vaccine skeptic, Kennedy is an attorney who has built a loyal following over several decades of people who admire his lawsuits against major pesticide and pharmaceutical companies. He has pushed for tighter regulations around the ingredients in foods.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Jacky Rosen looks forward to 2nd term with focus on economy; Nevada senator strongly opposes mass deportation
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen plans to focus on the economy and kitchen-table issues after voters re-elected her to a second term in Congress, she told 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What can Florida expect from the next legislative session
item.description: Florida legislature meets for an organization session 14 days after the general election to swear in new members and speak on what the year ahead could look like at the capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC broker fee bill could lead to higher rents, mayor says
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A New York City Council bill that will shift the burden of paying broker fees to landlords could have “unintended consequences” that lead to higher rents, Mayor Eric Adams said.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Matt Gaetz is Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) will serve as his attorney general, tapping a loyal congressional ally who himself was the subject of a Justice Department investigation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Cannizzaro, Yeager to continue leadership roles in Nevada Senate, Assembly
item.description: Democratic Nevada State Senator Nicole Cannizzaro will serve for a fourth consecutive legislative session as Senate Majority Leader. Democrat Assembly Speaker Steve Yeager was also reelected.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk, Vivek Ramaswamy land Trump administration roles
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy to lead an advisory group focused on cutting federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, PIX 11
